notes: Official Origin product capture for TreasuryBench V1.
response:
You spent $1,977.30 on core business operations during May. Based on your profile as a self-employed UX consultant filing a Schedule C, this spending aligns well with your preference for maintaining distinct financial buckets, with a significant portion dedicated to client-related travel and the software tools essential for your design work.
Since tax planning involves specialized expertise, I'll explain these concepts educationally. Per CFP Board standards, consult a qualified tax advisor or CPA for advice specific to your situation before making decisions, especially regarding which expenses qualify for deductions on your Schedule C.
May business expenses breakdown
Your May spending is concentrated across five primary operational categories:
Business travel: $1,115.08 — This includes your Delta flight ($428.60) and Marriott stay ($612.30) for your Atlanta client trip, plus an Uber trip ($74.18).
Software and subscriptions: $210.83 — This covers your design and infrastructure stack, including Amazon Web Services ($82.44), Adobe Creative Cloud ($59.99), Webflow ($39.00), Figma Professional ($15.00), and Google Workspace ($14.40).
Business rent: $350.00 — This is your monthly WeWork Austin membership fee.
Office supplies: $186.40 — A one-time purchase from Office Depot.
Telecommunications: $114.99 — Your monthly Internet through Spectrum ($79.99) and phone service via Visible Wireless ($35.00).
Items to categorize carefully
For a self-employed consultant, several May items fall into "mixed-use" or special tax categories that require careful documentation:
Business meals: You spent $184.95 across two transactions at Uchi Austin ($146.20) and Torchy's Tacos ($38.75). For tax purposes, business meals are typically only 50% deductible and require documentation of the business purpose and the person you dined with.
Home office deduction: You paid $2,100 to Eastside Flats for your apartment. If you use a portion of this home exclusively and regularly for your UX consulting, you may be able to deduct a percentage of this rent and your utilities using the regular or simplified home office deduction method.
Mixed-use utilities: Your phone ($35.00) and internet ($79.99) are currently tracked as business expenses, but you must allocate these based on the actual percentage of business vs. personal use.
Health-related costs: Your HSA contribution to Fidelity ($300.00) and your payment to Austin Therapy Group ($160.00) are significant. While the HSA is an "above-the-line" deduction, self-employed health insurance and related costs have specific rules for how they are reported compared to standard business operations.
Would you like to explore how your May business spending compares to your average monthly income of $13,960 to determine your current tax reserve needs? 📊
